properties.name: The Shire
properties.description: Bilbo's birthday party; starting point for Frodo. This is a test update
properties.source: Fellowship of the Ring, Book 1

properties.name: Bree
properties.description: Bree and the Prancing Pony; meeting with Strider.
properties.source: Fellowship of the Ring, Book 1

properties.name: Weathertop Amon Sûl
properties.description: Attack by Ringwraiths; Frodo wounded.
properties.source: Fellowship of the Ring, Book 1

properties.name: Rivendell
properties.description: Council of Elrond; Fellowship formed.
properties.source: Fellowship of the Ring, Book 2

properties.name: Moria
properties.description: Fellowship crosses the Mines of Moria; Gandalf falls.
properties.source: Fellowship of the Ring, Book 2

properties.name: Lothlorien
properties.description: Fellowship rests and receives gifts.
properties.source: Fellowship of the Ring, Book 2

properties.name: Amon Hen
properties.description: Breaking of the Fellowship; Boromir slain.
properties.source: Fellowship of the Ring, Book 2

properties.name: Isengard
properties.description: Saruman's stronghold; later flooded by Ents.
properties.source: Fellowship of the Ring, Book 2

properties.name: Minas Tirith
properties.description: Capital of Gondor; later major battle.
properties.source: Return of the King, Book 5

properties.name: Mordor
properties.description: Mount Doom and Barad-dur; final events of the War of the Ring.
properties.source: Return of the King, Book 6

properties.name: Bilbo's Party and Departure
properties.description: Bilbo celebrates his 111th birthday, uses the Ring to disappear, and leaves the Shire, bequeathing the Ring to Frodo.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: Gandalf reveals the Ring's nature
properties.description: Gandalf explains the history and danger of the Ring to Frodo and urges him to leave the Shire.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: Frodo leaves Hobbiton
properties.description: Frodo departs Bag End with Sam and Pippin, beginning the journey out of the Shire.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: Travel through the Shire
properties.description: Frodo and companions travel across the Shire, encountering ordinary rural hazards and meeting Farmer Maggot later in the narrative.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: Conspiracy revealed at Bag End
properties.description: Frodo learns that his friends have known about the Ring and planned to help him; preparations to leave accelerate.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: The Old Forest and Tom Bombadil
properties.description: The hobbits enter the Old Forest, are trapped by Old Man Willow, and are rescued by Tom Bombadil.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: In the House of Tom Bombadil
properties.chapter: In the House of Tom Bombadil
properties.description: The hobbits stay with Tom Bombadil and Goldberry, learning more about the Old Forest and receiving hospitality.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: Fog on the Barrow-downs
properties.description: The hobbits are trapped by a barrow-wight on the Barrow-downs and are freed by Tom Bombadil, who arms them.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: At the Sign of the Prancing Pony
properties.chapter: At the Sign of the Prancing Pony
properties.description: The hobbits arrive in Bree and stay at the Prancing Pony, where they first encounter Aragorn (Strider) indirectly.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: Strider reveals himself
properties.description: Aragorn (Strider) meets the hobbits openly, offers guidance, and becomes their protector on the road.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: Weathertop attack
properties.description: At Weathertop (Amon Sûl) the hobbits and Aragorn are attacked by Ringwraiths; Frodo is wounded by a Morgul blade.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One

properties.name: Flight to the Ford of Bruinen
properties.description: Aragorn, Glorfindel (in some editions/interpretations), and the hobbits race to the Ford of Bruinen where Frodo is carried across and the Ringwraiths are swept away by the flood.
properties.source: The Fellowship of the Ring, Book One
